title: 第 61 節
en: — not to be accounted for, 172.
— applied to Kant's intuitions of sense and his categories, 173.
— and Mithya^wana, 185 — Nescience, 268, 284, 285, 378, — an actual power, Sakti, 280.
— origin of, 289.

Avi^a, not having a seed, 342. Avmabhava, Not -without -being, Aviruddhakos, 240. Avisesha, subtle elements, 34 in. Aviveka, 285, 367. Avividisha, carelessness, 266. Avn'shtfi, 269. Avyakta, 188, 246, 341 n., 372.
— producing, Prasuta, 245.
— doubtful meaning of, 78.
— chaos, 245 Awake, state of being, 174. Ayur-veda, 413.
BABAKA PEAVAHA^I, significative name, 208.
Babylonian hymns, more modern in thought than those of Kigveda, 34.
Badarayana, author of one of the Mimamsas, 85; 116, 120, 371.
— quotes Gaimini, 91, 198.
— identified with Vyasa, 113. Badari, referred to by Badar&yana, Bahutva, 271.

Bawa knows Kapilas, Kaiiadas, 241. Baza's Harsha&arita, 600 A. p., 241. Bandha, bondage, 272. Bandhas, or bindings, 349. Bante, Buddhist title, 16. Barhaspatyam, studied by Buddha, Bathing, (graduating) the pupil, Berkeley, 194. Bhadrasana, 349, Bhagavatas, followers of Kn'shwa, SiBhartnhari, date of death, 650 A. r>., — refers to the Darsanas, 90. Bhafta, 404.
Bhava, the real world, the cause of Samadhi, 343. Bhikkhu, name of, 236. BhikshaTtarya, or begging, 236.
— and Bhaiksha/carya, 236. Bhikshu- Sutras, loss of, referred to by Bhaskaraftarya, 86. Parasarya, the author, 97, same as Vedanta- Sutras, 117.
Bhikshus, mendicants, 24, 31.
Bhur, 150 Bhuta-sarga, 272.
Bhutadi, 249, 250.

Bhutatman, elementary Atman, Bimbisara, 16, 27. Boar— legend that it brought forth the earth, allusions in Brah- niawas, 73. Bod da, name found among followers of Mani, 64. Boddo (on coins), name of Buddha, Bodhayana, 117, 230. Body, a subtle and a gross, 300.
— Sarira, 416.
— is it the same at Atman, 416 Brahma, creator, 18.
— called Vasudeva, 188. Brahmadatta, 16. Brahma-^ala-sutta, 16, 17, 21. Brahman, various meanings of, 52.
— ife the sun, 142.
— is Manas, 142.
Brahman is food, 142.
— is Vigfftana, 142.
• — as the Word, the first creation of divine thought, 145, 149, 150, — or Va/c or Bn'h, eternal, 150.
— is everything, 172.
— as the Kantian Ding an sich, — is the world, 280.
